claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements scope validation and external research support with clear separation between project facts and external evidence.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four valid scopes: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: Establishes the core scope taxonomy that evidence agents must use

evidence.summary: Agent prompt generation includes explicit instructions for external research: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external'
evidence.why: Shows implementation of external research support with scope marking requirement

evidence.summary: Tool profile conditional logic: 'toolProfile.includes("research") ? "- WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts."'
evidence.why: Demonstrates research profile gets external fetch tools with explicit scope separation

evidence.summary: Tool profile normalization converts 'research', 'fetch', 'web', 'web-research', 'external-research' to 'research' profile
evidence.why: Shows how different research-related tool profiles are normalized for external research enablement

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() function validates that every evidence record declares scope as project, external, user, or unknown
evidence.why: Provides scope declaration validation that catches missing scope declarations

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function analyzes evidence records and determines scope from field patterns, including URL detection and source type analysis
evidence.why: Implements scope inference logic for processing various evidence record formats

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() function checks if record explicitly declares valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES
evidence.why: Core validation function for scope declaration compliance

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() function validates project scope records have repo-relative paths, not URLs
evidence.why: Ensures project evidence includes concrete file locators as required

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function generates blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with path locators
evidence.why: Enforces the core requirement that existing-project facts need project evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() function returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED errors for records without declared valid scope
evidence.why: Provides the validation mechanism that catches missing scope declarations

evidence.summary: PRD readiness validation includes checks for evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() for complete evidence results
evidence.why: Shows how scope validation is integrated into PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Documentation defines three evidence agent protocols: explorer (primary facts), cross-checker (independent verification), verifier (evidence sufficiency check)
evidence.why: Provides the doctrinal foundation for evidence agent roles and responsibilities

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'dispatch 不靠阉割 tools 做安全边界：agent 可以拥有审计、检索、fetch、命令和子任务等能力'
evidence.why: Explains the safety philosophy of allowing full tools while maintaining boundary control

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: '证据记录必须声明 scope: project | external | user | unknown. project 证据来自目标项目的 code、tests、docs、config、logs 或 artifacts，并且必须带 repo-relative path'
evidence.why: Establishes the doctrinal requirement for scope declaration and project evidence locators

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external 证据可以来自 WebFetch/WebSearch 或外部文档，但只能作为背景研究，不能单独证明目标项目已有字段、API、状态机或数据流'
evidence.why: Explicitly prohibits using external evidence as proof of existing project implementation

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() function creates before/after snapshots of project files, excluding allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Implements the boundary audit mechanism that detects any unauthorized project mutations

evidence.summary: Boundary audit compares before/after snapshots and generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any changes outside allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Enforces the boundary control by detecting any evidence agent project mutations

evidence.summary: Execution policy defines boundary: project_mutation: 'forbidden', allowed_write_roots: [repo-relative artifact directory]
evidence.why: Shows the explicit boundary policy in the dispatch plan

assumptions: WebFetch and WebSearch tools are available in the Claude provider environment when research profile is enabled
The current implementation correctly handles the distinction between scope declaration and scope validation
The boundary audit mechanism catches all file modifications outside the allowed artifact root
risks: If WebFetch/WebSearch tools are not available in the runtime environment, external research requests will fail silently
Scope inference logic in evidenceRecordScope() might not handle all possible evidence record formats
Boundary audit only checks file digests, not other potential mutations like environment variables or external state
missing: Test files that verify the research tool profile enables WebFetch/WebSearch tools in actual Claude provider execution
Test files that verify external evidence marked as scope=external cannot satisfy project fact requirements
Documentation or examples showing the correct format for external research evidence records
Evidence that the scope validation correctly handles edge cases like malformed URLs or ambiguous path formats

result.notes: Core implementation for scope validation and external research support exists in code and documentation. The separation between project facts (requiring path locators) and external evidence (WebFetch/WebSearch) is well-defined doctrinally and implemented through scope validation functions. Main gap is test coverage for actual provider tool availability and edge case handling.
